title: Stage Boundaries
use_for: splitting a system into explicit pipeline stages
assigning ownership to intermediate and final stage outputs
defining where one recompute region ends and another begins
rules: split stages around data dependency changes
make each stage produce a concrete owned output
avoid stage boundaries that exist only by naming convention
keep stage contracts narrow enough to review quickly
procedure: Group transformations by shared inputs and owned outputs.
Separate stages where recompute scope differs.
Name each boundary by the data it consumes and produces.

primary_when: the request is specifically about how to split a data-oriented pipeline into stages or boundaries
must_not_own_when: the request is mainly about runtime dispatch policy or invalidation strategy

solves: how to align data dependency changes with pipeline structure

core_principles: A stage boundary is justified by owned data, not by arbitrary code grouping.
Stage outputs should be inspectable and reusable.
Boundary shape should reduce recompute scope.
constraints: Stages must not depend on invisible downstream mutation.
Boundaries must not force unnecessary whole-pipeline reruns.
decision_rules: Split when a downstream product can stay valid despite an upstream local change elsewhere.
Avoid over-splitting if no owned intermediate product exists.

preferred_patterns: boundary design driven by recompute economics
avoid_patterns: stages that only forward mutable references around

recipes.name: Split a transform chain into stable stages
recipes.when: Use when one long chain recomputes too much after local change.
steps: Find where a reusable intermediate product appears.
Promote that product to an explicit stage output.
Stop downstream recompute when upstream changes do not invalidate it.

pitfalls: Leaving stage outputs mutable and shared everywhere.
Over-splitting a tiny pipeline into noise.
review_checklist: Does each stage own a real output?
Do stage boundaries reduce recompute scope?
Can downstream consumers name the stage product they depend on?

references.title: Local Reference: Stage Boundaries
references.interpretation: Use this first for stage splitting, owned products, and recompute boundary logic.
references.why: Use as the primary local guide for boundary design.
